tiny_practice.action: Breathe deeply and reflect on creating peace in your relationships, instead of stirring up conflict

two_step.reflect_question: How am I contributing to harmony or discord in my closest relationships?

options.response: Practice active listening and pause before responding defensively

options.text: I try to understand before being understood

options.response: Learn to let minor offenses go and seek reconciliation

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a picture of two people resolving a conflict peacefully, showing kindness instead of anger

reevaluation.question: How has reading Proverbs 17 shifted your view of conflict and relationships?
options: I see conflict as an opportunity for growth
I recognize the importance of restraint
I understand relationships matter more than being right
I want to develop more wisdom in communication

three_choice.question: Which core wisdom principle resonates most deeply with you?

choices.text: Peace is more valuable than prosperity
choices.expansion: Inner tranquility transcends material abundance and social status.

choices.text: Character reveals true worth, not circumstances
choices.expansion: Divine testing occurs through life's challenges, not external achievements.
Mocking the vulnerable reflects a fundamental spiritual disconnection.

socratic_nudge.question: If true wisdom is measured by how one responds to adversity, what uncomfortable truths are you currently avoiding?

moral_slider.question: How do you balance personal justice with mercy?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of conflict and character transformed?
options: I now see conflict as an opportunity for growth
Character matters more than external success

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Testing of Character
thematic_summaries.summary: God deeply examines human hearts beyond external circumstances. The refining process of character involves spiritual trials that reveal true spiritual condition and integrity.

thematic_summaries.title: Peace Over Prosperity
thematic_summaries.summary: Spiritual tranquility and relational harmony are more valuable than material abundance. Internal peace supersedes external wealth, emphasizing quality of relationships over quantity of possessions.

reevaluation.question: How does God's perspective on human character differ from human social measurements?
options: God evaluates internal motivations, not external achievements
Spiritual integrity matters more than social status
Divine testing reveals true character beyond performance
Relational harmony is a higher spiritual metric than material success
